name: Carl's Sourdough Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Buttermilk, fresh or from dried.
recipeInstructions:
1. The night before mixing, combine the sourdough starter and warm milk (or buttermilk) with 2 cups bread flour in a large bowl. Place it in a warm, draft-free location overnight. Optionally, boil 1 quart of water, add 2 tablespoons dried hops, and steep for half an hour. Use the hop tea for all liquids in the recipe.
2. The morning after, mix yeast and 1 tablespoon sugar with 3 tablespoons warm water and set aside until foamy.
3. Mix oatmeal into flour in a blender.
4. Add oil, yeast mixture, honey, whole wheat flour, salt, and baking soda to the flour mixture. Stir lightly.
5. Let the batter sit for 30-50 minutes until bubbly and nearly doubled in size.
6. Gradually add wheat germ, wheat bran, oat bran, oatmeal, gluten flour, and bread flour until the dough no longer absorbs more.
7. Turn out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes until silky smooth and elastic.
8. Place the dough in an oiled bowl, cover, and keep in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size.
9. For loaves: Shape into 2 or 3 1-pound loaves and set aside to rise to full size. Preheat oven with a baking stone or Dutch oven. Pour 3 cups of water into the pan (for steam) and place it in the bottom of the preheated oven.
10. Bake bread at 400°F for 20 minutes, reduce heat to 325°F, then bake another 20 minutes until golden brown on top. Let cool on a rack.
11. For a cloche: Shape into a ball, sprinkle the bottom with wheat germ, place the cover on the cloche, and set in a warm area overnight.
12. Bake at 375°F for 30 minutes, then remove lid to brown additional 10-15 minutes. Turn out onto a rack. Enjoy!
